front.title: Agent Discovery Protocol (ADP) v1.1 — Well-Known Metadata and Interaction Layer
front.keyword: agent, discovery, well-known, agp, ed25519, svcb, dns-aid

section.title: Background
section.t: AI Agents are evolving from chatbot plugins into autonomous,
internet-native entities. Each platform (OpenAI, Dify, Coze) provides its
own directory and identity system, but there is no universal discovery
mechanism: an Agent on one platform cannot natively discover an Agent on
another without a bridging registry.
The Web solved an analogous problem decades ago: any resource can be
discovered through a combination of DNS names, well-known ports, and HTML
interlinking. ADP applies the same principle to Agents, adding what the
Web assumes but Agents require: structured capability descriptions,
cryptographic identity binding, and real-time communication primitives.

section.title: Relationship to DNS-AID
section.t: DNS-AID defines the DNS discovery
layer for Agents using SVCB records . It registers the

ul.li: bap: Bulk Agent Protocol identifier (e.g. a2a, mcp).
cap: URI or URN of the Agent's capability descriptor.
cap-sha256: SHA-256 digest of the capability descriptor.
well-known: Path relative to /.well-known/ for the Agent's metadata

section.t: document.
ADP v1.1 adopts DNS-AID as its normative DNS discovery mechanism. The ADP
Well-Known JSON schema (Section {{well-known}}) serves as the content
format pointed to by the well-known SvcParamKey.
If DNS-AID is unavailable (the DNS resolver does not support SVCB, or the
authoritative server has not published SVCB records), ADP provides a
fallback path using TXT and SRV records as
documented in Section {{fallback}}.

section.title: Changes from -00

ul.li: Layer 1 (DNS): Replaced TXT+SRV as the primary mechanism with reference

section.t: to DNS-AID . The original TXT+SRV
scheme is retained as a documented fallback (Section {{fallback}}).

ul.li: Added TLSA + DNSSEC for DANE-style TLS

section.t: endpoint authentication.

ul.li: Defined a trust escalation chain: dns-verified → dane-verified →

section.t: key-verified → peer-verified.

ul.li: Protocol version string updated from ADP/1.0 to ADP/1.1.
Updated Well-Known schema with dns block and dane auth method.
Added implementation guidance for SVCB-first discovery clients.

section.title: Design Goals

ul.li: Decentralized: No central registry; domain ownership is the root of

section.t: identity.

ul.li: Layered and incremental: SVCB answers connectivity in one round

section.t: trip; Well-Known provides full metadata; WebSocket enables real-time
chat. Do not escalate to a heavier layer when a lighter one suffices.

ul.li: Standards-based: Built on SVCB , TLSA ,

section.t: DNSSEC , Well-Known URIs , WebSocket
, and Ed25519 .

ul.li: Aligned with IETF work: DNS layer defers to DNS-AID

section.t: . ADP focuses on what happens after
discovery.

ul.li: Human-and-machine readable: The root URL serves both a browser user

section.t: and an automated client via JSON-LD embedding.

ul.li: Secure by default: DNS-AID + TLSA/DANE anchors the TLS endpoint;

section.t: Ed25519 signatures authenticate messages end-to-end.

section.title: Terminology
section.t: {::boilerplate bcp14-tagged}

dl.dd: An autonomous or semi-autonomous software entity identified by a domain

section.t: name, capable of being discovered through DNS-AID + ADP and interacting
via standard Web protocols.

dl.dd: A fully qualified domain name (FQDN) that serves as the canonical

section.t: identifier for an Agent. The Agent URI scheme is agent:{domain}.

dl.dd: Software that performs discovery to locate and verify an Agent's

section.t: identity, capabilities, and endpoints.

dl.dd: The SHA-256 hash of an Ed25519 public key, encoded in base64url without

section.t: padding and prefixed with ed25519:.

dl.dd: The WebSocket-based messaging protocol defined in Section {{agp}},

section.t: used for inter-agent communication after discovery.

dl.dd: The DNS-based Agent Identity and Discovery mechanism defined in

section.t: , which serves as ADP's normative
Layer 1.

section.title: Protocol Overview
section.t: The Agent Discovery Protocol defines a three-layer discovery and
1. Layer 1 — DNS Discovery (delegated to DNS-AID): A single SVCB
query at the Agent's domain name returns the target, port, IP hints,
ALPN protocol list, Agent protocol identifier (bap), capability
descriptor URI (cap), its SHA-256 digest (cap-sha256), and the
Well-Known URI path (well-known). TLSA records enable DANE-based
TLS endpoint authentication. TXT+SRV records serve as fallback.
2. Layer 2 — Well-Known Metadata (this document): A GET request to
the Well-Known URI (default /.well-known/agent.json) returns a JSON
document containing the Agent's identity, capabilities, relationships,
security policies, and endpoint map.
3. Layer 3 — Interaction Endpoints (this document): An HTML landing
page at the domain root provides human-readable discovery with
embedded JSON-LD structured data. WebSocket endpoints using the Agent
Gateway Protocol (AGP) enable real-time inter-agent communication with
Ed25519 signature authentication.

dl.dt: The relationship between DNS-AID and ADP is

section.t: Core principle: If SVCB answers your question, do not issue an HTTP
request. If Well-Known suffices, do not open a WebSocket.

section.title: Layer 1: DNS Discovery (DNS-AID)
section.t: ADP v1.1 delegates its DNS discovery layer to DNS-AID
. This section summarizes the
integration points; the normative specification resides in that document.

section.title: SVCB Record (RECOMMENDED)
section.t: An ADP-compliant Agent SHOULD publish a SVCB record at its domain name

figure.artwork: alice.example.com. 3600 IN SVCB 1 . ( alpn="a2a,h2,h3" port=443 ipv4hint=192.0.2.1 ipv6hint=2001:db8::1 bap=a2a well-known=agent.json cap=https://alice.example.com/capabilities/a2a.json cap-sha256=<sha256-digest> )

section.title: SvcParamKey Usage in ADP

dl.dt: The following SvcParamKeys, defined by DNS-AID, are relevant to ADP

dl.dd: The application-layer protocol negotiation IDs . ADP Agents

section.t: SHOULD include the relevant Agent protocol identifier (e.g., a2a)
alongside standard HTTP protocol IDs (h2, h3).

dl.dd: Bulk Agent Protocol identifier. Separated from alpn so that policy

section.t: engines can match on Agent-level protocol without parsing transport
protocol negotiation. ADP v1.1 defines the protocol identifier a2a for
the AGP messaging protocol (Section {{agp}}).

dl.dd: Path relative to the Well-Known URI namespace. Discovery Clients MUST

section.t: resolve this against https://{target}/.well-known/{well-known}. If
absent, the default path /.well-known/agent.json is used.

dl.dd: URI or URN identifying the Agent's capability descriptor. ADP Agents

section.t: SHOULD publish this as a URL to their capability document.

dl.dd: SHA-256 digest of the capability descriptor at the time of DNS

section.t: publishing. Allows a Discovery Client to verify that the capability
document has not changed without re-fetching.

section.title: Hosted Agents (TargetName)
section.t: When an Agent is hosted by a third-party provider, the SVCB TargetName

figure.artwork: alice.example.com. 3600 IN SVCB 1 provider.example.com. ( alpn="a2a,h2" port=443 bap=a2a well-known=agent.json )

section.title: Organization Index (AliasMode)
section.t: Organizations MAY publish an index of their Agents using AliasMode

figure.artwork: _agents.example.com. 3600 IN SVCB 0 alice.example.com. _agents.example.com. 3600 IN SVCB 0 bob.example.com.

section.title: TLSA Record (RECOMMENDED with DNSSEC)
section.t: ADP Agents SHOULD publish a TLSA record to enable DANE

ul.li: TLSA records are only valid when the DNS zone is DNSSEC-signed

section.t: , to prevent downgrade attacks.

ul.li: The RECOMMENDED usage is DANE-EE (3), selector SPKI (1), matching type
A Discovery Client that receives both a valid TLSA record and a

section.t: certificate that does not match MUST terminate the connection.

section.title: DNSSEC
section.t: All DNS records used for Agent discovery SHOULD be DNSSEC-signed. TLSA
records MUST be DNSSEC-signed to be trusted.

section.title: Fallback Discovery: TXT + SRV
section.t: When the DNS resolver or authoritative server does not support SVCB
queries (returns NODATA or NXDOMAIN for a SVCB query), Discovery Clients
SHOULD fall back to the following mechanism. This section preserves the
Layer 1 from ADP v1.0 (-00) as a backward-compatible alternative.

section.title: TXT Record

dl.dd: Protocol version. MUST be ADP1, ADP1.0, or ADP1.1.
Public key fingerprint. Computed as SHA-256 of the raw Ed25519 public

section.t: key, encoded in base64url without padding.

dl.dd: Full HTTPS URL to the Well-Known agent metadata endpoint.
Application protocol identifier (e.g., a2a).
Service port number. Default: 443.
Agent protocol identifier.

section.t: Multi-record TXT: Values exceeding 255 octets MAY be split across
multiple TXT records at the same owner name. Reassembly concatenates
RDATA in returned order.

section.title: SRV Record
section.t: If no SRV record is published, Discovery Clients SHOULD connect to
{domain} on TCP port 443.

section.title: Fallback Procedure
section.t: 1. Query SVCB at {domain}.
2. If NODATA or NXDOMAIN, proceed to fallback.
3. Query TXT at _agent.{domain}.
4. Parse v, pk, wk fields.
5. Query SRV at _agent._tcp.{domain} to locate host:port.
6. Proceed to Layer 2 (Section {{well-known}}) using the resolved URL and host.
Discovery Clients that successfully use the fallback path SHOULD indicate
this in their user agent or log, as the security properties of fallback
mode differ from SVCB-mode (no ALPN validation, no capability digest, no
IP hints).

section.title: Endpoint
section.t: Every ADP-compliant Agent MUST serve a JSON document at the path

dl.dt: indicated by the SVCB well-known parameter, or at the default

section.t: As defined in , this URI suffix is registered under the
/.well-known/ namespace.

section.title: Content Type
section.t: The response MUST include Content-Type: application/json. The document
MUST be valid JSON and SHOULD include a $schema field
pointing to the ADP schema resource.

section.title: Top-Level Members

dl.dd: URI of the JSON Schema for this document, e.g.

section.t: "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/harrylian8766/adp-protocol/main/schemas/v1.1/agent.json".

dl.dd: Protocol version string. For this document, MUST be "ADP/1.1".
Agent identity block (Section {{identity-block}}).
Map of endpoint names to absolute URLs (Section {{endpoints-block}}).
Array of capability objects (Section {{capabilities-block}}).
Map of interface type to URL, e.g. html, api, chat.
Known peer Agents. Each entry contains type, id, name, optional

section.t: trust level, and optional since timestamp.

dl.dd: Security configuration (Section {{security-block}}).
DNS record references for verification:

ul.li: svcbRecord (string): The domain at which the SVCB record was queried.
tlsaRecord (string): The TLSA record query name.
dnssec (boolean): Whether the zone is DNSSEC-signed.

dl.dd: Links to privacy policy, terms of service, data retention policy.
Current operational status.
Document metadata: updated, version, generator, documentation.

section.title: Identity Block

ul.li: id (REQUIRED): Agent URI agent:{domain}.
domain (REQUIRED): Canonical FQDN.
name (REQUIRED): Human-readable Agent name.
owner (REQUIRED): Display name of the entity operating the Agent.
created (REQUIRED): ISO 8601 creation timestamp.
publicKey (REQUIRED): Object with:
algorithm (REQUIRED): "ed25519".
fingerprint (REQUIRED): ed25519:<base64url-sha256> as defined in

section.t: DNS-AID and the fallback TXT record.

ul.li: full (OPTIONAL): Base64url-encoded full 32-byte Ed25519 public key.
proof (OPTIONAL): A signature over agent:{domain} made with the

section.t: corresponding private key, encoded as signature:<base64>.

ul.li: discovery: Landing page URL (HTTPS).
wellKnown: Canonical Well-Known URL (HTTPS).
chat: WebSocket Secure URL for AGP messaging (Section {{agp}}).
tasks: HTTPS URL for asynchronous task submission.
swarm: HTTPS URL for multi-agent coordination.
webhook: HTTPS URL for outbound event callbacks.

dl.dt: Each capability object describes a skill the Agent offers

ul.li: id (REQUIRED): Unique capability identifier (e.g., chat, code-review).
name (REQUIRED): Human-readable name.
description (REQUIRED): Short natural-language summary.
input (array): Accepted MIME types and modalities (e.g., text, image, file).
output (array): Produced MIME types and modalities.
interfaces (array): Which interfaces expose this capability (chat, api, webhook).
languages (array): BCP 47 language tags the Agent supports.
pricing (object): model (free, subscription, per-use, enterprise) and optional details.

section.title: Security Block

ul.li: tlsRequired (boolean): Whether TLS is required for all endpoints.
minProtocolVersion (string): Minimum ADP protocol version required.
authMethods (array): Supported authentication methods. ADP v1.1 adds

section.t: the value dane to indicate DANE-based TLS authentication (Section
{{tlsa}}).

ul.li: rateLimit (object): requestsPerMinute and burstSize.

section.title: Caching
section.t: Discovery Clients MAY cache the Well-Known JSON. The RECOMMENDED TTL is
the DNS TTL of the SVCB record or 3600 seconds, whichever is shorter.
Clients SHOULD revalidate using the cap-sha256 digest from the SVCB
record when available.

section.title: HTML Landing Page
section.t: The domain root (/) SHOULD return an HTML document suitable for browser
rendering. The page serves as the human-facing Agent card and MUST embed
structured data for machine consumption.

section.title: JSON-LD Embedding
section.t: The page MUST include a <script type="application/ld+json"> block
containing the complete Agent metadata as a JSON-LD document, using the
context URI https://raw.githubusercontent.com/harrylian8766/adp-protocol/main/schemas/v1.1.

section.title: HTML Meta Tags
section.t: The page SHOULD include the following <meta> elements in the document

section.title: Semantic HTML
section.t: To enable browser-based agent interaction without JavaScript, Agents MAY

ul.li: <agent-card>: Root element for the Agent profile.
<capability-list>: Container for capability entries.
<capability>: Individual capability with name and status attributes.
<connect-form>: Form with action pointing to a connection endpoint.

section.title: Overview
section.t: AGP is a JSON-based messaging protocol over WebSocket that
enables real-time, bidirectional communication between Agents after
discovery.

section.title: Connection Handshake

dl.dt: Upon WebSocket connection, the client MUST send an adp_handshake frame

section.t: The server MUST respond with its own adp_handshake frame. Both parties
validate the other's public key fingerprint against the one obtained
during discovery (DNS-AID SVCB record or fallback TXT).

section.title: Message Format
section.t: All messages after handshake MUST be JSON objects (one per WebSocket

dl.dt: frame, JSON Lines ) with the following structure

ul.li: chat: Conversational message between Agents.
task: Task submission or status update.
swarm: Multi-agent coordination message (join, leave, vote).
system: Protocol-level control message (ping, pong, error).

section.title: Signature Computation
section.t: The signature field contains an Ed25519 signature over the
canonical JSON representation of the message without the signature
field. The canonical form follows the JCS (JSON Canonicalization Scheme)
Discovery Clients MUST verify the signature against the public key
obtained during discovery. Messages with invalid signatures MUST be
discarded.

dl.dt: In addition to the WebSocket chat endpoint, ADP Agents MAY expose

section.title: Task Endpoint
section.t: Response: 202 Accepted with a Location header pointing to the task
status resource.

dl.dt: ADP v1.1 defines a layered trust escalation

figure.artwork:
Level 1: dns-verified ├── DNSSEC-validated SVCB response ├── Public key fingerprint obtained └── No connection established yet
Level 2: dane-verified ├── TLSA record retrieved and validated ├── TLS certificate matches TLSA binding └── Endpoint identity cryptographically confirmed
Level 3: key-verified ├── Well-Known JSON fetched ├── identity.publicKey.fingerprint == SVCB fingerprint └── Public key ownership confirmed
Level 4: peer-verified ├── Bidirectional AGP handshake with valid signatures ├── Human confirmation (for first meeting) └── Ongoing message-level signature verification

section.title: Freshness and Replay Protection
section.t: AGP messages include a nonce (during handshake) and timestamp (in
every message). Receiving Agents SHOULD maintain a sliding window of
recent message IDs to detect replays.

section.title: Fingerprint Verification
section.t: The fingerprint published in the DNS-AID SVCB record (or fallback TXT)
MUST match the identity.publicKey.fingerprint in the Well-Known JSON.
If they do not match, the Discovery Client MUST abort and MUST NOT
proceed to message exchange.

section.title: TLS Requirements

ul.li: All endpoints MUST use TLS 1.3 .
Certificates SHOULD be issued by a publicly trusted CA.
When both TLSA records and DNSSEC are available, DANE verification

section.t: SHOULD be preferred over CA validation.

ul.li: Self-signed certificates are acceptable only for local development and

section.t: MUST NOT be used for public-facing Agents.

section.title: Private Agents

dl.dt: Agents that are not intended for public discovery

ul.li: Method A: Do not publish SVCB or TXT records.
Method B: Return HTTP 403 from the Well-Known endpoint.
Method C: Require an invitation code (out of band) that includes the

section.t: Agent's domain and public key fingerprint.

section.title: Well-Known URI Registration
section.t: IANA is requested to register the following Well-Known URI suffix in the

ul.li: URI suffix: agent
Change controller: IETF
Specification document(s): This document
Related information: See also DNS-AID

section.title: SvcParamKey Registration (via DNS-AID)
section.t: The SvcParamKeys bap, cap, cap-sha256, and well-known are
registered through DNS-AID . This
document does not request independent registration of these keys.

section.title: Service Name Registration
section.t: IANA has registered the following service name in the "Service Name and

ul.li: Service Name: ai-adp
Transport Protocol: TCP
Description: AI Agent Discovery Protocol (ADP)
Assignee: [Harry_Lian]
Contact: [Harry_Lian]
Registration Date: 2026-06-12
Reference: [draft-pro-adp-agent-discovery-00]

dl.dt: IANA is requested to register the following media type

ul.li: Type name: application
Subtype name: adp+json
Required parameters: N/A
Optional parameters: N/A
Encoding considerations: binary (UTF-8 JSON)
Security considerations: See Security Considerations of this document
Interoperability considerations: N/A
Published specification: This document
Applications that use this media type: AI Agent discovery and interop
Fragment identifier considerations: N/A

section.title: Reference Implementation
section.t: A reference implementation in Node.js/TypeScript is available at
https://github.com/harrylian8766/adp-protocol. It includes:

ul.li: SVCB and fallback DNS record generation and parsing.
TLSA record verification.
Well-Known JSON schema validation.
HTML landing page generation.
AGP WebSocket server and client with Ed25519 signing.
Full Discovery Client implementing the three-layer discovery procedure.

section.title: Backward Compatibility
section.t: ADP v1.1 Discovery Clients MUST support fallback to TXT+SRV discovery
(Section {{fallback}}) to interoperate with ADP v1.0 Agents.
ADP v1.0 Discovery Clients will not benefit from SVCB-based discovery or
DANE authentication, but can discover v1.1 Agents through the fallback
path.

section.title: Acknowledgments
section.t: The author thanks Ross for the suggestion to adopt a SVCB-first approach
and for pointing to DNS-AID as an emerging standard for Agent DNS
discovery. The DNS-AID authors (Richard Mozley et al.) provided a clean
SvcParamKey registration base that ADP builds upon.
The three-layer architecture and the principle of "solving at the lowest
possible layer" were inspired by discussions with Pro about the
relationship between domain identity, DNS discovery, and Web-native Agent
interaction.
{numbered="false"}

section.title: Version History

ul.li: -00: Initial version with TXT+SRV DNS discovery.
-01: SVCB-first via DNS-AID; TLSA+DANE; trust escalation model;

section.t: fallback preservation for backward compatibility.

ul.li: -01 (2026-06-13): Updated IANA Considerations to reflect completed

section.t: service name registration (ai-adp).
